reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in TDX guest port I/O emulation code, confirming that both patches are relevant to the issues at hand.
reviews.raw_body:
Link: https://lore.kernel.org/all/CAKw_Dz96rfSQc6Rn+9QBcUFHhmkK+9zu+P=bxowfZwxrATCBRg@mail.gmail.com/

It is relevant for both.

-- 
  Kiryl Shutsemau / Kirill A. Shutemov

2026-04-01.patch_summary: This patch series addresses two technical inaccuracies in the TDX guest port I/O emulation code, specifically an off-by-one error in GENMASK() macro usage and incorrect zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O operations. The patches fix these issues by correcting the mask calculation and ensuring that the result is correctly extended to a full 64-bit RAX register. These changes were introduced in the initial implementation of runtime hypercalls for port I/O and are necessary to maintain accurate emulation of x86 architecture requirements.
